title: • Today's Intention: I will identify one cycle in my life (a habit, a t
content:
• Today's Intention: I will identify one cycle in my life (a habit, a thought pattern) that I wish to break.
    • NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "He had been driven into this Sansara, this cycle, by a thirst, a foolish thirst, and he had ceased to be a player and had become a die in the game."
    • Reflection: Looking back at the week, what patterns emerged? What was the "game" I was playing, and did I enjoy it?
    Week 3: The River's Wisdom
    Weekly Goals:
    • Goal 1:
    • Goal 2:
    • Goal 3:
    Day 15
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "He had a dream. In this dream, Kamala’s little songbird... was dead. It lay stiff on the floor of the cage... he was terribly shocked, and his heart ached as if he had thrown away with this dead bird all that was good and of value in himself."
    • Today's Intention: I will pay attention to my "dreams," both sleeping and waking, for messages from my deeper self.
    NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "A shudder went through his body. He felt that something had died in him, that something had gone from him, that he was alone and empty."
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 18 (chunk 18/29)
